steps.prompt: Which word is the main clause finite verb?

hints.debuimus: 'Debuimus' is a finite verb but belongs to the 'quod' clause, not the main clause
hints.fecimus: 'Fecimus' is a finite verb but belongs to the 'quod' clause

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'simus' — 'sumus' is indicative

hints.sumo, sumere, sumpsi, sumptum: These are the parts of 'sumere' (to take)
hints.sero, serere, sevi, satum: These are the parts of 'serere' (to sow)
hints.sequor, sequi, secutus sum, —: These are the parts of 'sequi' (to follow)

steps.reveal: debuimus = we owed / we were obliged

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'debemus' — 'debuimus' has the perfect stem 'debu-'
hints.pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect would be 'debueramus' — 'debuimus' is simple perfect

hints.dubito, dubitare, dubitavi, dubitatum: These are the parts of 'dubitare' (to doubt)
hints.debilito, debilitare, debilitavi, debili: These are the parts of 'debilitare' (to weaken)
hints.habeo, habere, habui, habitum: These are the parts of 'habere' (to have) — similar conjugation pattern

hints.genitive: Genitive singular is also 'servi' — but here it is nominative plural (predicate with 'sumus')
hints.vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'servi' — but here it is a predicate nominative, not an address
hints.dative: Dative would be 'servis' — 'servi' is nominative plural

hints.accusative: 'Inutiles' could be accusative plural, but here it modifies 'servi' as predicate — nominative
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'inutilibus' — 'inutiles' is nominative/accusative
hints.vocative: Vocative could look the same, but 'servi inutiles sumus' is a statement, not an address

translation: We are unprofitable servants; what we were obliged to do, we have done
